email_sequence.subject: Water utility ops audit — Medford Water Dept.
email_sequence.body:
Hi Scott,

I'm reaching out because Raybern specializes in operational audits for water utilities running Tyler MUNIS — and we've found a consistent pattern of underutilization and integration gaps at utilities with a similar profile to Medford Water Dept..

I also noticed Medford Water Dept. has 2 open EPA violations on record. That's often a symptom of data pipeline friction, not operational negligence — and it's exactly the kind of thing we untangle.

We recently completed an engagement at a MA utility where we identified over $200K in unbilled consumption tied directly to a Sensus FlexNet integration failure. The system "worked" — but the data wasn't making it through to billing.

Would a 20-minute call make sense to see if there's a fit?

Best,
[Your Name]
Raybern

email_sequence.subject: Re: Medford Water Dept. — one specific question
email_sequence.body:
Hi Scott,

Following up quickly — I'll make this specific:

Is your team confident that every meter read from your Sensus FlexNet system is being captured correctly in Tyler MUNIS?

We ask because most utilities we audit aren't. The integration looks healthy on the surface, but there's almost always a gap — and utilities don't know until they do a full reconciliation.

Happy to share what we found at a comparable utility if that's useful.

[Your Name]
Raybern

email_sequence.subject: Last note — Medford Water Dept.
email_sequence.body:
Hi Scott,

One last note, then I'll leave you alone.

We put together a short summary of the three most common issues we find at utilities running Tyler MUNIS + Sensus FlexNet. If any of them ring a bell, it's worth a quick call.

Happy to send it over — just reply "send it" and I'll get it to you.

[Your Name]
Raybern

email_sequence.subject: Water utility ops audit — Mdwasa/Rex Utilities
email_sequence.body:
Hi Mwasd,

I'm reaching out because Raybern specializes in operational audits for water utilities running Oracle — and we've found a consistent pattern of underutilization and integration gaps at utilities with a similar profile to Mdwasa/Rex Utilities.

I also noticed Mdwasa/Rex Utilities has 22 open EPA violations on record. That's often a symptom of data pipeline friction, not operational negligence — and it's exactly the kind of thing we untangle.

We recently completed an engagement at a FL utility where we identified over $200K in unbilled consumption tied directly to a Itron integration failure. The system "worked" — but the data wasn't making it through to billing.

Would a 20-minute call make sense to see if there's a fit?

Best,
[Your Name]
Raybern

Hi Ralph,

email_sequence.subject: Water utility ops audit — Weir River Water System
email_sequence.body:
Hi Russell,

I'm reaching out because Raybern specializes in operational audits for water utilities running Munis — and we've found a consistent pattern of underutilization and integration gaps at utilities with a similar profile to Weir River Water System.

I also noticed Weir River Water System has 38 open EPA violations on record. That's often a symptom of data pipeline friction, not operational negligence — and it's exactly the kind of thing we untangle.

We recently completed an engagement at a MA utility where we identified over $200K in unbilled consumption tied directly to a Sensus FlexNet integration failure. The system "worked" — but the data wasn't making it through to billing.

Would a 20-minute call make sense to see if there's a fit?

Best,
[Your Name]
Raybern

email_sequence.subject: Re: Weir River Water System — one specific question
email_sequence.body:
Hi Russell,

Following up quickly — I'll make this specific:

Is your team confident that every meter read from your Sensus FlexNet system is being captured correctly in Munis?

We ask because most utilities we audit aren't. The integration looks healthy on the surface, but there's almost always a gap — and utilities don't know until they do a full reconciliation.

Happy to share what we found at a comparable utility if that's useful.

[Your Name]
Raybern

email_sequence.subject: Last note — Weir River Water System
email_sequence.body:
Hi Russell,

One last note, then I'll leave you alone.

We put together a short summary of the three most common issues we find at utilities running Munis + Sensus FlexNet. If any of them ring a bell, it's worth a quick call.

Happy to send it over — just reply "send it" and I'll get it to you.

email_sequence.subject: Water utility ops audit — Cambridge Water Department
email_sequence.body:
Hi Julie,

I'm reaching out because Raybern specializes in operational audits for water utilities running PeopleSoft — and we've found a consistent pattern of underutilization and integration gaps at utilities with a similar profile to Cambridge Water Department.

I also noticed Cambridge Water Department has 76 open EPA violations on record. That's often a symptom of data pipeline friction, not operational negligence — and it's exactly the kind of thing we untangle.

We recently completed an engagement at a MA utility where we identified over $200K in unbilled consumption tied directly to a Sensus FlexNet integration failure. The system "worked" — but the data wasn't making it through to billing.

Would a 20-minute call make sense to see if there's a fit?

Best,
[Your Name]
Raybern

email_sequence.subject: Re: Cambridge Water Department — one specific question
email_sequence.body:
Hi Julie,

Following up quickly — I'll make this specific:

Is your team confident that every meter read from your Sensus FlexNet system is being captured correctly in PeopleSoft?

We ask because most utilities we audit aren't. The integration looks healthy on the surface, but there's almost always a gap — and utilities don't know until they do a full reconciliation.

Happy to share what we found at a comparable utility if that's useful.

[Your Name]
Raybern

email_sequence.subject: Water utility ops audit — Billerica Water Works
email_sequence.body:
Hi Todd,

I'm reaching out because Raybern specializes in operational audits for water utilities running Tyler MUNIS — and we've found a consistent pattern of underutilization and integration gaps at utilities with a similar profile to Billerica Water Works.

I also noticed Billerica Water Works has 6 open EPA violations on record. That's often a symptom of data pipeline friction, not operational negligence — and it's exactly the kind of thing we untangle.

We recently completed an engagement at a MA utility where we identified over $200K in unbilled consumption tied directly to a Sensus FlexNet integration failure. The system "worked" — but the data wasn't making it through to billing.

Would a 20-minute call make sense to see if there's a fit?

Best,
[Your Name]
Raybern

email_sequence.subject: Re: Billerica Water Works — one specific question
email_sequence.body:
Hi Todd,

Following up quickly — I'll make this specific:

Is your team confident that every meter read from your Sensus FlexNet system is being captured correctly in Tyler MUNIS?

We ask because most utilities we audit aren't. The integration looks healthy on the surface, but there's almost always a gap — and utilities don't know until they do a full reconciliation.

Happy to share what we found at a comparable utility if that's useful.

[Your Name]
Raybern

email_sequence.subject: Last note — Billerica Water Works
email_sequence.body:
Hi Todd,

One last note, then I'll leave you alone.

We put together a short summary of the three most common issues we find at utilities running Tyler MUNIS + Sensus FlexNet. If any of them ring a bell, it's worth a quick call.

Happy to send it over — just reply "send it" and I'll get it to you.

[Your Name]
Raybern

email_sequence.subject: Water utility ops audit — Lake Mitchell Water Incorporated
email_sequence.body:
Hi Honea,

I'm reaching out because Raybern specializes in operational audits for water utilities running Nexbillpay — and we've found a consistent pattern of underutilization and integration gaps at utilities with a similar profile to Lake Mitchell Water Incorporated.



We recently completed an engagement at a AL utility where we identified over $200K in unbilled consumption tied directly to a your AMI infrastructure integration failure. The system "worked" — but the data wasn't making it through to billing.

Would a 20-minute call make sense to see if there's a fit?

Best,
[Your Name]
Raybern

email_sequence.subject: Re: Lake Mitchell Water Incorporated — one specific question
email_sequence.body:
Hi Honea,

Following up quickly — I'll make this specific:

Is your team confident that every meter read from your your AMI infrastructure system is being captured correctly in Nexbillpay?

We ask because most utilities we audit aren't. The integration looks healthy on the surface, but there's almost always a gap — and utilities don't know until they do a full reconciliation.

Happy to share what we found at a comparable utility if that's useful.

[Your Name]
Raybern

email_sequence.subject: Last note — Lake Mitchell Water Incorporated
email_sequence.body:
Hi Honea,

One last note, then I'll leave you alone.

We put together a short summary of the three most common issues we find at utilities running Nexbillpay + your AMI infrastructure. If any of them ring a bell, it's worth a quick call.
